jokes.text: Hi, I'm your hostess Boobarella, I hope you'll enjoy our labour day marathon of murder because soon you'll have to go back to school. School!!! School? My god I wasted the whole summer.
jokes.setup: TV host cheerfully reminds viewers about returning to school
jokes.punchline: Bart's panicked realization he's wasted his entire summer
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart character moment - the relatable panic of wasted summer time, well-timed and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Now Simpsons be careful, their kid pitcher's mount is really 22.
jokes.setup: Warning about a little league baseball game
jokes.punchline: The 'kid' pitcher is actually 22 years old
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor about competitive parents in youth sports - economical and unexpected

jokes.text: Sit on it, Springfield!
jokes.setup: Reference to Happy Days catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Generic application to Springfield
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference without much added wit - functional but not creative

jokes.text: Get outta my face, you raggedy maggots! So that's why everyone's been saying that all summer.
jokes.setup: Bart hears aggressive phrase from movie/TV
jokes.punchline: Realizes this explains a summer trend he didn't understand
jokes.explanation: Relatable moment about how pop culture phrases spread - decent observation comedy

jokes.text: I just had my first kiss. When you get to third base, I'll buy you a beer.
jokes.setup: Bart innocently announces his first kiss
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate parenting response about sexual advancement and alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting moment - inappropriate but character-consistent bad advice delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Weaving? Homer, you're my father. You're supposed to protect me from things like this. All right. Just this once, you can take a drink out of my special water bottle.
jokes.setup: Bart complains about boring museum weaving exhibit
jokes.punchline: Homer's solution is to offer Bart alcohol from his 'special water bottle'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer solution - terrible parenting disguised as understanding, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Die, weapons, die! Don't start the exhibit without me!
jokes.setup: Bart pretends to attack weapons while running
jokes.punchline: Homer desperately calling for Bart not to miss the exhibit
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Homer's childish enthusiasm matching Bart's

jokes.text: Time to do what I do best, ruin it for everybody.
jokes.setup: Homer sees a long line at the museum
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-aware declaration of his destructive nature
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware character moment - Homer acknowledging his chaos-causing nature with pride

jokes.text: You calling me a liar in front of my kids? 'Cause I'd take a bullet for you, man, right in the mouth!
jokes.setup: Homer confronts Ned about cutting in line
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre declaration of loyalty involving taking a bullet 'in the mouth'
jokes.explanation: Absurd Homer logic - aggressive confrontation immediately followed by bizarre loyalty pledge

jokes.text: Ned Flanders is doling out cuts. I, for one, wish to partake of said!
jokes.setup: Seeing Homer get away with cutting in line
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's pretentious way of asking for cuts using 'partake of said'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy voice - unnecessarily formal language for a simple request

jokes.text: Hey, if boner here is gettin' cuts, I want 'em, too.
jokes.setup: Chain reaction of people wanting to cut in line
jokes.punchline: Barney's crude nickname for Comic Book Guy
jokes.explanation: Simple character-appropriate crude humor - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Disco Stu slides in front of you! That rhymes.
jokes.setup: Disco Stu cuts in line
jokes.punchline: His pleased recognition that his statement rhymed
jokes.explanation: Classic Disco Stu - his childish delight in accidental rhyming perfectly fits the character

jokes.text: If we turn around, it will feel like we're at the front of the line!
jokes.setup: Homer's chaos has created a backwards line situation
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical solution to the problem he created
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - creating more chaos as a solution to existing chaos

jokes.text: Artifacts of the time indicate that this incident was hilarious.
jokes.setup: Educational film about ancient nunchuck invention
jokes.punchline: Deadpan historical analysis claiming ancient people found it funny
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan historical narration - treating ancient slapstick as serious historical fact

jokes.text: It's a mugger! I'll hand over all my hugs and kisses.
jokes.setup: Baby Maggie appears threatening with weapon
jokes.punchline: Marge's sweet misinterpretation of being 'mugged' for affection
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - Marge's maternal instinct to find innocence in threatening situation

jokes.text: Do you want to play in the NBA or not? Bart, my arm's out of its thing. That means it's working.
jokes.setup: Bart is apparently injuring Milhouse during 'training'
jokes.punchline: Bart's twisted logic that injury means the training is effective
jokes.explanation: Good Bart/Milhouse dynamic - Bart's callous treatment of his best friend for dubious goals

jokes.text: It's narrated by Melanie Griffith. This room's nice. Let's go in to the next one. This room's nice, too. This room's nice. This room's nice, too. This room's nice. This room's nice, too.
jokes.setup: Homer gives away his audio tour narrated by Melanie Griffith
jokes.punchline: The repetitive, vapid nature of her commentary
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity parody - captures the vapid nature of some celebrity endorsements with repetitive humor

jokes.text: My most cherished beliefs, a myth? Daddy, was mommy a monkey? I can't remember.
jokes.setup: Ned is having a crisis about evolution challenging his beliefs
jokes.punchline: His child's innocent but devastating question about Maude being a monkey
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - child's innocent question hitting Ned at his most vulnerable moment

jokes.text: No one was ever a monkey! Everything is what it was and always will be! God put us here and that's that. But you said a stork brought me. That was god disguised as a stork. Who brings baby storks? There's no such thing as storks! It's all god.
jokes.setup: Ned trying to explain creationism to his confused child
jokes.punchline: The logical spiral leading to 'there's no such thing as storks! It's all god!'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity - Ned's religious fundamentalism taken to its logical extreme, creating increasingly ridiculous explanations

jokes.text: Well... our membership has been dwindling since those episcopalians put in those vibrating pews.
jokes.setup: Discussion about declining church membership
jokes.punchline: Competition from Episcopalians with vibrating pews (sexual innuendo)
jokes.explanation: Clever sexual innuendo disguised as church innovation - economical and unexpected

jokes.text: I'll be a white Al Sharpton.
jokes.setup: Ned getting excited about mobilizing his religious community
jokes.punchline: His comparison to Al Sharpton but with racial qualifier
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Ned's naive enthusiasm, though the racial comparison is somewhat dated

jokes.text: No! That was a once-in-a-lifetime APR!
jokes.setup: Church threatening Skinner's car lease if he doesn't comply
jokes.punchline: Skinner's panic about losing his special financing rate
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner character moment - his bureaucratic priorities trumping educational principles

jokes.text: Are oceans god's tears? They sure are. A-plus. Now Lisa's the Ralph.
jokes.setup: Creationism class with absurd questions getting A+ answers
jokes.punchline: Lisa being reduced to Ralph's intellectual level by the new system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant role reversal joke - academic Lisa being equated with slow Ralph due to system change

jokes.text: Then why is he making out with satan?
jokes.setup: Lisa defending Darwin's scientific contributions
jokes.punchline: Anti-evolution propaganda showing Darwin kissing Satan
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag concept - absurd propaganda imagery as 'evidence' against evolution

jokes.text: The George will?
jokes.setup: Lisa mentioning conservative George Will accepts evolution
jokes.punchline: Skinner's shocked recognition of the name
jokes.explanation: Decent reaction beat showing even conservative authorities accept evolution

jokes.text: Children, drown her out with whistling and arm flatulence.
jokes.setup: Lisa continuing to argue for evolution
jokes.punchline: Skinner's ridiculous method to silence her with childish noises
jokes.explanation: Absurd authority figure behavior - principal encouraging disruption to avoid intellectual discussion

jokes.text: Today we had a test, and every answer was 'god did it.'
jokes.setup: Lisa complaining about creationism class
jokes.punchline: The reductive nature of the curriculum reducing everything to one answer
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about reductive religious education - economical and pointed

jokes.text: My son's a brat, but he's a special little guy.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining how incompatible things can both be true
jokes.punchline: Her example using contradictory statements about Bart
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge character moment - finding diplomatic way to acknowledge Bart's flaws while being loving

jokes.text: You father says he's at work, but there he is jumping on a trampoline.
jokes.setup: Marge continuing examples of contradictory truths
jokes.punchline: Visual of Homer supposedly at work but actually playing
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer character consistency - visual gag of him being caught in obvious lie about working

jokes.text: Good riddance. Their drawings suck and they look like hippies.
jokes.setup: Discussion about no cavemen in creationism
jokes.punchline: Bart's superficial complaints about cavemen
jokes.explanation: Good Bart character moment - his shallow aesthetic objections to prehistoric humans

jokes.text: Onions?! In the peas?! What the hell!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to have an open mind to new ideas
jokes.punchline: His violent reaction to onions mixed with peas
jokes.explanation: Perfect character hypocrisy moment - Homer's claim of open-mindedness immediately contradicted by food rage

jokes.text: regular people can park in handicapped spaces if they're just running in to buy smokes or maybe some pop tarts for tomorrow. The system works.
jokes.setup: Mayor Quimby concluding some official business
jokes.punchline: Corrupt ruling allowing handicapped space abuse for trivial purchases
jokes.explanation: Good political corruption humor - absurd official ruling that benefits lazy citizens

jokes.text: Now, honestly, the bible's about the same age as, the rest of our textbooks.
jokes.setup: Lisa arguing against teaching creationism
jokes.punchline: Deadpan observation about Springfield's outdated textbooks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan social commentary - equating ancient religious text with Springfield's neglected educational system

jokes.text: Homer's son's sister's right.
jokes.setup: Moe agreeing with Lisa's argument
jokes.punchline: His unnecessarily complex way of identifying Lisa
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment - Moe's roundabout way of speaking showing his social awkwardness

jokes.text: while I beat this ominous drum.
jokes.setup: Skinner collecting evolution books
jokes.punchline: His self-aware acknowledgment of the dramatic drumbeat
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor - character acknowledging the dramatic musical accompaniment to his actions

jokes.text: Little Moe Szyslak? Why does everybody call me that? Bunch of snot-faced pukebags.
jokes.setup: Mystery of who 'L.M.S.' could be
jokes.punchline: Moe's hurt feelings about his diminutive nickname and crude response
jokes.explanation: Good Moe character moment - his insecurity and crude overreaction to perceived slight

jokes.text: I thought we're gonna light spiders on fire. We're over here.
jokes.setup: Bullies thought they were coming to different kind of meeting
jokes.punchline: Their expectation of animal cruelty instead of intellectual discussion
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate dark humor - bullies preferring violent entertainment over learning

jokes.text: We only have the manpower to enforce the last law passed. You know, it's not the best system, but... well, in fact, it's... it's pretty much the worst.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning why they're enforcing this particular law
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's honest assessment of their terrible law enforcement system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum moment - his bumbling honesty about incompetent police procedures

jokes.text: You live! You die! You live! You die! In the old days, we would've been all over that.
jokes.setup: Police ignoring actual violent crime happening nearby
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic acknowledgment of when they'd actually respond to real crimes
jokes.explanation: Good police incompetence humor - wistful remembrance of when they did actual police work

jokes.text: As the little chicken said to his mama, 'I just hope I don't 'cluck up.''
jokes.setup: Country lawyer introduction
jokes.punchline: Terrible chicken pun
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad pun fits character but isn't particularly funny

jokes.text: This is Kent Brockman, juror number seven, saying, 'I'll have the roast beef for lunch.'
jokes.setup: Kent reporting on the trial he's also a juror in
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate restaurant order while supposedly being journalistic
jokes.explanation: Good media ethics joke - news anchor's conflict of interest and unprofessional behavior

jokes.text: It just says that god is an impotent nothing from nowhere with less power than the undersecretary of agriculture, who has very little power in our system.
jokes.setup: Lawyer explaining evolution doesn't necessarily deny God
jokes.punchline: Accidentally devastating description of God's powerlessness with bureaucratic comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating insult disguised as explanation - bureaucratic comparison makes it funnier

jokes.text: It just says that god is an impotent nothing from nowhere with less power than the undersecretary of agriculture, who has very little power in our system.
jokes.setup: Asked if evolution means no god
jokes.punchline: Increasingly insulting description ending with bureaucratic comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating insult with bureaucratic punchline

jokes.text: I have a phd in truthology from christian tech.
jokes.setup: Witness establishing credentials
jokes.punchline: Made-up degree from biased institution
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pseudo-academic credentials

jokes.text: Why, I believe, right now, this gentleman's having dinner with the tooth fairy and the easter bunny. This is the break in the case we've been waiting for! I mean, he doesn't exist.
jokes.setup: Discussing the missing link in evolution
jokes.punchline: Lawyer's confusion about whether the missing link exists or not, getting excited then correcting himself
jokes.explanation: Decent courtroom confusion - lawyer's brief excitement before realizing his mistake

jokes.text: Now. Bambi, who started that forest fire that killed your mama? Evolution?! My, my, my. Poor bambi. Poor thumper...
jokes.setup: Lawyer using Disney characters as witnesses
jokes.punchline: Blaming evolution for Bambi's mother's death in a forest fire
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist courtroom moment - treating cartoon characters as real witnesses and blaming evolution for unrelated tragedy

jokes.text: Wow, Darwin's argument is incredibly persuasive, and his ship was the beagle, which reminds me of snoopy, my favorite peanut!
jokes.setup: Homer reading about Darwin
jokes.punchline: Stream of consciousness from Darwin to Snoopy to Peanuts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer brain - legitimate learning derailed by word association

jokes.text: Your new name: Senorita Ugly.
jokes.setup: Milhouse suggesting Lisa flee to Mexico
jokes.punchline: His terrible choice of fake identity name
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate bad plan - Milhouse's insulting and obvious fake identity suggestion

jokes.text: You can take off the wig, Mihouse. I'm Nelson!
jokes.setup: Assumed Milhouse was wearing disguise
jokes.punchline: Actually Nelson in disguise
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection gag, functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: I'm Nelson!
jokes.setup: Someone told to take off their wig, assumed to be Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be Nelson in disguise
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection gag - unexpected character reveal, economically delivered

jokes.text: It's not a twist-off! Come on!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to open beer during testimony
jokes.punchline: Struggling with bottle cap while serious testimony continues
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer priorities - beer struggle during important moment

jokes.text: It's not a twist-off! Come on! I want what's in you! stupid... Homer, I'm trying to... will you shut your yap, you big monkey-faced gorilla!
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with beer bottle during important trial testimony
jokes.punchline: Marge's frustrated insult calling him a 'monkey-faced gorilla' during evolution trial
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup/payoff - Marge accidentally proves evolution by calling Homer a gorilla during the evolution trial, with perfect comedic timing

jokes.text: will you shut your yap, you big monkey-faced gorilla!
jokes.setup: Evolution trial proceeding while Homer struggles with bottle
jokes.punchline: Marge accidentally using evolution insult in evolution trial
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic moment - Marge inadvertently proves evolution while trying to stop Homer's disruption

jokes.text: Wait, it was a twist-off.
jokes.setup: Homer's dramatic struggle with the beer bottle throughout the scene
jokes.punchline: His quiet realization that he was doing it wrong the whole time
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimatic ending to the running gag - Homer's understated realization after all the drama

jokes.text: Maybe a bird with a people face or a bear with pants on!
jokes.setup: Marge expressing excitement about evolution making new things
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre and impossible evolutionary expectations
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's sweet but naive understanding of evolution

jokes.text: Cram it, ya god wad.
jokes.setup: Ned suggesting they all go out for ice cream together
jokes.punchline: Bart's crude rejection of Ned's wholesome offer
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart moment - crude insult delivered with perfect timing to deflate wholesome moment
